ddls:
CREATE TABLE "territorial_authorities" (
  "authority_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each territorial authority */,
  "authority_name" TEXT /* Name of the territorial authority */,
  "region" TEXT /* Region in which the authority is located */,
  "authority_type" TEXT /* Type of territorial authority */,
  "latitude" REAL /* Latitude of the territorial authority */,
  "longitude" REAL /* Longitude of the territorial authority */,
  "area_in_sq_km" REAL /* Area of the territorial authority in square kilometers */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("authority_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "demographics" (
  "demographic_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each demographic entry */,
  "authority_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the territorial authority */,
  "topic" TEXT /* Topic of the demographic data */,
  "measure" TEXT /* Specific measure being reported */,
  "category" TEXT /* Category of the measure */,
  "value" REAL /* Value of the measure */,
  "value_unit" TEXT /* Unit of the value */,
  "value_label" TEXT /* Label describing the value */,
  "null_reason" TEXT /* Reason for null value (if applicable) */,
  "date" INTEGER /* Year of the measurement */,
  "date_label" TEXT /* Label for the date */,
  "source" TEXT /* Source of the data */,
  "median_age" REAL /* Median age of the population */,
  "population_density" REAL /* Population density */,
  "data_quality" TEXT /* Data quality indicator */,
  "updated_date" TEXT /* Date when the demographic data was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("demographic_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_demographics_source FOREIGN KEY ("source") REFERENCES data_sources ("source_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_demographics_category FOREIGN KEY ("category") REFERENCES categories ("category_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_demographics_authority_id FOREIGN KEY ("authority_id") REFERENCES territorial_authorities ("authority_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_demographics_measure FOREIGN KEY ("measure") REFERENCES measures ("measure_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "measures" (
  "measure_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each measure */,
  "measure_name" TEXT /* Name of the measure */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of what the measure represents */,
  "measure_type" TEXT /* Type of measure */,
  "unit_of_measurement" TEXT /* Unit of measurement for the measure */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("measure_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "categories" (
  "category_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each category */,
  "category_name" TEXT /* Name of the category */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the category */,
  "category_group" TEXT /* Higher-level category group */,
  "subcategory" TEXT /* Subcategory for more specific classification */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("category_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "data_sources" (
  "source_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each source */,
  "source_name" TEXT /* Name of the data source */,
  "url" TEXT /* URL to the data source for reference */,
  "source_type" TEXT /* Type of data source */,
  "publication_date" TEXT /* Publication date of the data source */,
  "doi" TEXT /* Digital Object Identifier (DOI) for the data source */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("source_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user */,
  "institution" TEXT /* Institution or organization affiliation of the user */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Date and time of the last login */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "demographic_id" INTEGER /* ID of the demographic data being accessed */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the data */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the data was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access */,
  "access_ip" TEXT /* IP address of the user accessing the data */,
  "access_device" TEXT /* Type of device used to access the data */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_demographic_id FOREIGN KEY ("demographic_id") REFERENCES demographics ("demographic_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)
